{{Short description="Range of all types of electromagnetic radiation}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Electromagnetic spectrum
| image = EM spectrum.svg
| caption = The electromagnetic spectrum, from low-frequency [[radio wave]]s to high-frequency [[gamma ray]]s.
| topics = [[Electromagnetic wave]], [[Photon]], [[Frequency]], [[Wavelength]], [[Speed of light]]
}}

The '''electromagnetic spectrum''' is the range of all possible [[frequency|frequencies]] of [[electromagnetic radiation]]. It extends from low frequencies (long [[wavelength]]s) such as [[radio wave]]s to high frequencies (short wavelengths) such as [[gamma ray]]s. All electromagnetic waves travel at the [[speed of light]] in vacuum and are related by:

:<math>c = \lambda\nu</math>

where <math>c = 299,792,458</math> m/s, <math>\lambda</math> is the wavelength, and <math>\nu</math> is the frequency.

== Regions of the spectrum ==
{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Region !! Wavelength !! Frequency !! Photon energy
|-
| [[Radio wave]]s || > 10 cm || < 3 GHz || < 10⁻⁵ eV
|-
| [[Microwave]]s || 1 mm – 10 cm || 3 GHz – 300 GHz || 10⁻⁵ – 10⁻³ eV
|-
| [[Infrared]] || 750 nm – 1 mm || 300 GHz – 400 THz || 10⁻³ – 1.7 eV
|-
| [[Visible light]] || 380–750 nm || 400–790 THz || 1.7–3.3 eV
|-
| [[Ultraviolet]] || 10 nm – 380 nm || 790 THz – 30 PHz || 3.3–124 eV
|-
| [[X-ray]]s || 0.01–10 nm || 30 PHz – 30 EHz || 100 eV – 100 keV
|-
| [[Gamma ray]]s || < 0.01 nm || > 30 EHz || > 100 keV
|}

== Photon energy ==
The energy of a [[photon]] is proportional to its frequency:
:<math>E = h\nu = \frac{hc}{\lambda}</math>

where <math>h</math> is the [[Planck constant]]. Higher frequency corresponds to higher photon energy and more energetic interactions with matter.

== Propagation ==
All electromagnetic waves:
* Travel at <math>c</math> in vacuum
* Do not require a medium to propagate
* Are [[transverse wave]]s (<math>\mathbf{E} \perp \mathbf{B} \perp \hat{\mathbf{k}}</math>)
* Can exhibit [[reflection (physics)|reflection]], [[refraction]], [[diffraction]], [[interference (wave propagation)|interference]], and [[polarization (waves)|polarization]]

== Atmospheric windows ==
The [[Earth's atmosphere]] blocks much of the electromagnetic spectrum. Only two major "windows" reach the surface:
* '''Optical window''' — visible and near-infrared
* '''Radio window''' — radio waves from ~1 cm to ~10 m

This is why [[radio telescope]]s and [[optical telescope]]s are ground-based, while X-ray and gamma-ray astronomy requires space-based observatories.

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Electromagnetic wave]]
* [[Frequency]]
* [[Gamma ray]]
* [[Infrared]]
* [[Microwave]]
* [[Radio wave]]
* [[Ultraviolet]]
* [[Visible light]]
* [[X-ray]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:Electromagnetic spectrum| ]]
[[Category:Electromagnetic radiation]]
[[Category:Waves]]
